titleStmt.title: Letter from Private F. Murphy, 3 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: F. Murphy

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Private F Murphy to an unknown recipient. In the letter Murphy pleads with the recipient to get his children back, although it is not fully clear where the children are one can assume that that had been put in a home while Murphy was off fighting because the family had little money. Murphy describes how he and his wife wish to have them back now that he is home and he asks for the recipients help in the matter.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3922

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
3/1/16 6228 Private
F Murphy
3 R MF GCoy
Agda Camp
Co Cork
Ireland Dear Sir I beg to inform you
about my Chrelden as
I came to the conclusion
to take them out as I
made that statement
to the govenment to
take them out I have
got married and she
is inclined to look after
my Chrelden she have
got two Chrelden and
she is paying for one
of them to Constable
Neas
for one of them it is
not easy to pay out
of 91 shillings and to
keep herself I was sick
myself and I found the
balance of pay but as
bad as I was I Volteered
to fight for my Cortry
I was Invailed home
last March and was
4 Months in Cilvan
life and was called on
again so Dear Sir I
would be pleased to
know if you could let
me have my Chrelden
me Wife is Number 14
Frances Street Tralee
Co Kerry Name Anan Dear Sir Chreldenfour and
the Wife two in Tralee
homes
